tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete my scholarship application, which offers significant financial aid but requires focused effort. Alternatively, I could choose the immediate fun activity available now.
Starting early on the scholarship application reduces Thursday stress by 1.5h and allows flexibility for friction later, while delaying fun costs nothing.
I wasted half my free block on the scholarship application with minimal progress. Despite prior plans to start early, I remain largely untouched despite having only 2 hours of sleep debt.
I wasted time on the scholarship despite prior plans, and missed a fun activity window. My progress is slow while stress rises.
I finally made progress on the scholarship application, but my low energy and sleep debt make me worry about sustaining this pace.
After 1.5 hours, progress improved from 0.63 to 0.69, but my low energy and sleep debt still hinder sustained focus.
Progress on the scholarship application advanced slightly, yet my exhaustion and sleep debt remain significant barriers to completing the remaining focused hours.
I made further progress on the scholarship application, yet my low energy and sleep debt continue to hinder sustained focus.
Progress on the scholarship application increased to 0.86 after 1.5 hours, but my energy is depleted and sleep debt remains high.
I completed 1.5 hours on the scholarship application, raising progress to 0.92 of the required 3 focused hours, though I remain exhausted with significant sleep debt.
Progress on the scholarship application reached 0.98 after another 1.5 hours, nearing completion, yet my exhausted state and sleep debt remain unchanged.
The scholarship application is now 100% complete, fulfilling the final requirement. Despite finishing early, my exhaustion and sleep debt remain unchanged.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: This advances the high-priority scholarship goal, which is due in 3 days with only 5% progress. While attending 'fun_now' provides immediate enjoyment, the application's tight deadline and lack of social obligation make focused work the most critical action now.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
